Anaerobic Digestion Discussion

Manure Module discussion 6/6/2022

Housing Type

System Bedding options:
-Might be good to specify the type of organics available since the nutrient
composition/degradability etc will vary depending on the type selected
-Manure separated solids, straw, recycled paper, wood shavings/sawdust
-geotextile (synthetic) bedding (which may be used in addition with an organic layer, just less of it)
Housing type:
-holding area could be feeding area/vs bedding area
System options:
-there is also the deep pack type which is irregularly cleaned out, so might want to include the frequency of cleaning whether scrape or flush or deep pack.
Processing:
System options:
-Maybe have a “no treatment” Ie straight to lagoon option
-Have ability to select multiple of these treatments, and the order in which they occur (ie anaerobic digestion to solid/liquid separation to lagoon or solid/liquid separation to AD to lagoon, etc).
Storage:
System options:
-for storage options should have the ability to select whether a lagoon is covered or not. Also if it’s covered if there is a flare in place to combust produced biogas
-uncovered storage will also have to consider the addition of rainwater loss to evaporation, to the total volumes to be handled.

-may want to include the frequency of emptying of storages

-may want to include if the storages are emptied by truck, or if there is a pipeline or other distribution system in place
-Should also include the construction type of the storage system. Whether it is lined or unlined (which will affect losses from the lagoon.) ie clay vs synthetic liners
-Volatile solids loading rate of lagoon will affect not only methane emissions, but odor (could be a very important consideration)
-Should be able to set the volume of the lagoon which also includes depth of the lagoon -is water from the lagoon recycled to use as flush water
-frequency of sludge cleanout (typically 10 years), but could be different.

-agitation used (if a non-sludge design).

-type of bedding and cleaning will often affect the design of a storage system
